Package microui.component

Class TextView

java.lang.Object
microui.core.base.View
microui.core.base.SpatialView
microui.core.base.ContentView
microui.core.base.Component
microui.component.TextView
All Implemented Interfaces:
Visible
public final class TextView extends Component
A component for displaying text with automatic resizing, alignment, and clipping options. TextView provides flexible text rendering with support for automatic font size adjustment to fit within bounds, horizontal and vertical alignment, and optional text clipping.

  • Nested Class Summary

    Nested classes/interfaces inherited from class microui.core.base.SpatialView

    SpatialView.HooksUpdateMode

  • Constructor Summary

    Constructors
    Constructor
    Description
    TextView()
    Constructs a TextView with empty text, centered on the screen.
    TextView(float x, float y, float width, float height)
    Constructs a TextView with specified position and dimensions but no initial text.
    TextView(String text)
    Constructs a TextView with specified text, centered on the screen.
    TextView(String text, float x, float y, float width, float height)
    Constructs a TextView with specified text, position, and dimensions.

  • Constructor Details

    • TextView

      public TextView(String text, float x, float y, float width, float height)
      Constructs a TextView with specified text, position, and dimensions. The text view is initialized with automatic resizing enabled and centered alignment.
      Parameters:
      text - the text to display
      x - the x-coordinate of the text view's top-left corner
      y - the y-coordinate of the text view's top-left corner
      width - the width of the text view
      height - the height of the text view

    • TextView

      public TextView(float x, float y, float width, float height)
      Constructs a TextView with specified position and dimensions but no initial text.
      Parameters:
      x - the x-coordinate of the text view's top-left corner
      y - the y-coordinate of the text view's top-left corner
      width - the width of the text view
      height - the height of the text view

    • TextView

      public TextView(String text)
      Constructs a TextView with specified text, centered on the screen.
      Parameters:
      text - the text to display

    • TextView

      public TextView()
      Constructs a TextView with empty text, centered on the screen.

  • Method Details

    • getAlignX

      public int getAlignX()
      Gets the horizontal alignment of the text.
      Returns:
      the horizontal alignment (LEFT, CENTER, or RIGHT)

    • setAlignX

      public void setAlignX(int alignX)
      Sets the horizontal alignment of the text.
      Parameters:
      alignX - the horizontal alignment (must be LEFT, CENTER, or RIGHT)
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if alignX is not LEFT, CENTER, or RIGHT

    • getAlignY

      public int getAlignY()
      Gets the vertical alignment of the text.
      Returns:
      the vertical alignment (TOP, CENTER, or BOTTOM)

    • setAlignY

      public void setAlignY(int alignY)
      Sets the vertical alignment of the text.
      Parameters:
      alignY - the vertical alignment (must be TOP, CENTER, or BOTTOM)
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if alignY is not TOP, CENTER, or BOTTOM

    • isClipModeEnabled

      public boolean isClipModeEnabled()
      Checks if text clipping to bounds is enabled.
      Returns:
      true if text is clipped to component bounds, false if text can overflow

    • setClipModeEnabled

      public TextView setClipModeEnabled(boolean clipModeEnabled)
      Enables or disables text clipping to bounds.
      Parameters:
      clipModeEnabled - true to clip text to component bounds, false to allow overflow
      Returns:
      this TextView instance for method chaining

    • getTextSize

      public float getTextSize()
      Gets the current text size.
      Returns:
      the current manual text size in pixels

    • setTextSize

      public void setTextSize(float textSize)
      Sets the manual text size (used when auto-resize is disabled).
      Parameters:
      textSize - the text size in pixels (must be greater than 0)
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if textSize is less than or equal to 0

    • getFont

      public processing.core.PFont getFont()
      Gets the current font.
      Returns:
      the current font, or null if using default font

    • setFont

      public void setFont(processing.core.PFont font)
      Sets the font for text rendering.
      Parameters:
      font - the font to use for text
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if font is null

    • getText

      public String getText()
      Gets the current text content.
      Returns:
      the current text string

    • setText

      public void setText(String text)
      Sets the text content to display.
      Parameters:
      text - the text to display
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if text is null

    • isAutoResizeModeEnabled

      public boolean isAutoResizeModeEnabled()
      Checks if automatic text resizing is enabled.
      Returns:
      true if auto-resize is enabled, false if using manual text size

    • setAutoResizeModeEnabled

      public void setAutoResizeModeEnabled(boolean autoResizeModeEnabled)
      Enables or disables automatic text resizing. When enabled, text size is automatically calculated to fit within component bounds.
      Parameters:
      autoResizeModeEnabled - true to enable auto-resize, false to use manual text size

    • getAutoResizeMode

      public AutoResizeMode getAutoResizeMode()
      Gets the auto-resize mode (determines how aggressively text is resized).
      Returns:
      the current auto-resize mode

    • setAutoResizeMode

      public void setAutoResizeMode(AutoResizeMode autoResizeMode)
      Sets the auto-resize mode (determines how aggressively text is resized).
      Parameters:
      autoResizeMode - the auto-resize mode to use

    • getTextColor

      public AbstractColor getTextColor()
      Gets the text color.
      Returns:
      the current text color

    • setTextColor

      public void setTextColor(AbstractColor textColor)
      Sets the text color.
      Parameters:
      textColor - the color for text rendering
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if textColor is null
